seo.title: Luma Ray 2 Flash Specs, Pricing and Legacy Draft Workflows | MaxVideoAI
seo.description: Use Luma Ray 2 Flash in MaxVideoAI as a previous-generation fast Luma route for older drafts, image-to-video tests, source-video modify passes, and social reframes. Start new Luma edit and reframe work on Ray 3.2.

overview: Luma Ray 2 Flash is a previous-generation fast Luma route in MaxVideoAI. Keep it for older Ray 2 Flash drafts and comparison context, while Ray 3.2 is the current Luma route for new Modify, keyframe, and Reframe work.

hero.title: Luma Ray 2 Flash - faster drafts, modify, and reframe
hero.intro: Use Ray 2 Flash when you need to revisit older fast Luma drafts or Ray 2 Flash-compatible source-video exploration. For new Luma edits, guide/keyframe work, and Reframe delivery, start on Ray 3.2.
hero.badge: Legacy fast Luma route - T2V + I2V + Modify + Reframe

ctaPrimary.label: Start generating with Luma Ray 2 Flash

technicalOverview.label: Generate workflows
technicalOverview.body: Text-to-Video and Image-to-Video with 5 s or 9 s clips

technicalOverview.label: Edit workflows
technicalOverview.body: Modify and Reframe use an uploaded source video instead of fixed 5 s or 9 s presets

technicalOverview.label: Aspect ratios
technicalOverview.body: 16:9, 9:16, 4:3, 3:4, 21:9, and 9:21 on generate; Reframe also adds 1:1

technicalOverview.label: Image controls
technicalOverview.body: Image-to-Video supports one start image plus an optional end image; Modify and Reframe can also take an optional reference still

technicalOverview.label: Modify options
technicalOverview.body: Modify presets exposed in MaxVideoAI: adhere_1 to adhere_3, flex_1 to flex_3, and reimagine_1 to reimagine_3

technicalOverview.label: Reframe options
technicalOverview.body: Target aspect ratio plus optional grid anchors and crop-window coordinates

technicalOverview.label: Pricing
technicalOverview.body: Displayed pricing starts around $0.20 for 5 s at 540p on generate, about $0.12/s for Modify, and about $0.06/s for Reframe

promptStructure.title: How to prompt Luma Ray 2 Flash by workflow
promptStructure.quote: Mode: Reframe. Goal: repurpose the approved landscape teaser into a 9:16 mobile cut. Priority subject: the product in the upper-center third. Keep the existing timing and motion continuity.
promptStructure.description: Flash is best used as a draft and throughput engine, but the prompting logic stays workflow-specific. The instruction should match whether you are generating a new shot, animating a still, modifying a source clip, or reframing it for another placement.
steps: For Text-to-Video, keep the brief compact and visual: one subject, one move, one mood.
For Image-to-Video, let the start image anchor the shot and use the prompt to describe motion, energy, and landing.
Use an end image only when you want tighter closing control in a draft pass.
For Modify, tell Flash what must stay from the source clip and what should change, then choose the right modify preset.
For Reframe, prioritize subject placement and delivery format instead of rewriting the whole scene.
Use Flash for iteration speed first, then move the approved prompt and workflow to Ray 2 when you need a premium final.

tips.title: Tips and limits
items: Use Flash to shortlist concepts before moving the winner to Ray 2.
Modify is ideal when the timing and camera path already work but the look still needs exploration.
Reframe is the fastest way to turn one approved master into mobile and square variants.
Keep one strong motion beat per generate run; Flash performs best when the shot idea stays simple.
Treat Flash as the throughput layer of the Luma family, not as a separate workflow philosophy.

prompts.title: Fast tunnel reveal
prompts.prompt: Futuristic sports car entering a wet tunnel, quick cinematic push-in, specular reflections on the asphalt, bold contrast, teaser energy, 5s, 16:9.

prompts.title: Animatic from still
prompts.prompt: Animate the uploaded storyboard frame into a short previs shot with clean parallax, readable subject motion, and restrained lighting transitions, 5s.

prompts.title: Fast modify pass
prompts.prompt: Keep the source clip camera path and timing, shift the setting into a cleaner showroom look, brighter key light, simpler background, flex_1.

prompts.title: Square commerce reframe
prompts.prompt: Reframe the uploaded product teaser into a 1:1 square crop, keep the bottle centered and fully readable across the full move.

faqs.q: What is the difference between Ray 2 and Ray 2 Flash now?
faqs.a: The workflow surface is aligned on purpose. Both models expose generate, modify, and reframe, but Ray 2 is the premium tier while Flash is optimized for faster drafts and lower-cost iteration.

faqs.q: Does Flash still support Modify and Reframe?
faqs.a: Yes. Flash exposes the same core Luma workflow family as Ray 2: text-to-video, image-to-video, modify, and reframe.

faqs.q: Which options are exposed in Flash Modify and Reframe?
faqs.a: Modify keeps adhere, flex, and reimagine presets. Reframe keeps target aspect ratio, optional prompt, optional reference image, and optional crop-window controls.

faqs.q: When should I stay on Flash instead of switching to Ray 2?
faqs.a: Stay on Flash while you are still exploring direction, pacing, crop strategy, or source-video edit strength. Move to Ray 2 when you want the higher-confidence premium pass.

custom.heroDesc1: Luma Ray 2 Flash now follows the same workflow-complete pattern as the stronger LTX and Veo pages in MaxVideoAI. It is still a two-model family, but each model now exposes the core Luma workflow routes instead of hiding the source-video tools.
custom.heroDesc2: That gives the family a cleaner product story. Ray 2 stays premium, Flash stays fast, and both models support net-new generation plus source-video modify and reframe workflows.
custom.heroSupportLine: Best for fast iteration when you still need real modify and reframe tools inside the same Luma family.
custom.whyTitle: Why Luma Ray 2 Flash is strategically useful
heroHighlights: Fast tier, full workflow story||Flash is no longer just a reduced draft page. It now exposes the same Luma workflow set as Ray 2: generate, modify, and reframe.
Better iteration economics||Generate pricing starts lower, which makes Flash the right place to test prompt direction, modify strength, and crop strategy.
Delivery edits without premium spend||Reframe stays useful even when the final creative is not ready for the flagship tier yet.
Clean upgrade path||Once the concept, modify preset, or crop strategy is approved, the team can move the exact workflow to Ray 2.
custom.specTitle: Real specs - Luma Ray 2 Flash in MaxVideoAI
custom.specNote: These specs describe the active Luma routing exposed in MaxVideoAI today, not a trimmed placeholder page.

specSections.title: Generate workflows
specSections.intro: Text-to-Video and Image-to-Video are the draft creation modes: 5 or 9 seconds, 540p to 1080p, multiple cinematic aspect ratios, optional loop, and optional end-image control.
items: Use Text-to-Video for fast concept exploration.
Use Image-to-Video when the first frame should stay close to an approved still.
Add an end image only when you need a guided landing.
Use loop for ambient, teaser, or product motion drafts.

specSections.title: Modify and Reframe
specSections.intro: Flash now exposes the same source-video tools as Ray 2, which makes it much more useful during early-stage iteration and delivery planning.
items: Modify explores look changes while keeping the original timing and camera path.
Adhere, flex, and reimagine presets let you choose how far the edit should drift from the source clip.
Reframe handles square and vertical delivery variants from one approved master.
Optional crop and grid controls keep the priority subject inside the new frame.

custom.whatTitle: How Luma Ray 2 Flash works in MaxVideoAI
custom.whatIntro1: Choose Luma Ray 2 Flash, then stay in Generate Video for text or still-led drafts, or switch to Modify / Reframe when you are working from a source clip.
custom.whatIntro2: This keeps the family easy to understand while exposing the real workflows where they matter: inside the model page and workspace.
whatFlowSteps: Pick Luma Ray 2 Flash as the engine.
Stay on Generate Video for text and image workflows, or switch to Modify / Reframe for source-video edits.
On generate runs, choose 5s or 9s, then pick resolution and aspect ratio.
Upload the relevant asset for the active workflow: start image, optional end image, or source video.
For Modify, pick the right adherence or reimagine preset.
Use Flash to iterate on direction, then move the approved workflow to Ray 2 if a premium final is needed.
strengths: Fast throughput while keeping the real Luma workflow surface.
Useful for source-video experimentation before premium finals.
Cleaner family story than exposing extra public Luma pseudo-models for every edit route.
boundaries: Generate durations and resolutions still apply only to the creation modes, not to source-video edit lengths.
Modify and Reframe quality still depends on the clarity of the uploaded source clip.
Ray 2 remains the better choice for the highest-confidence premium final.
custom.finalPara1: Luma Ray 2 Flash is the right fast counterpart to Ray 2 only if it exposes the same real workflows. That is now the case: simpler model lineup, stronger workflow coverage, and a cleaner path from draft to premium final.

hero.title: Luma Ray 2 Flash
hero.subtitle: Previous-generation fast Luma route for older Ray 2 draft coverage, Modify and Reframe.
hero.paragraph: Use Luma Ray 2 Flash when you need a legacy-compatible fast Ray 2 pass or older draft context. For new Luma source-video edits, keyframe guidance and Reframe work, start with Ray 3.2 instead of making Flash the first choice.

features.title: Legacy fast route

features.title: Reframe variants

features.title: Ray 3.2 for current edits

decisionCards.title: Flash or Ray 3.2?
decisionCards.body: Use Ray 3.2 for current Luma Modify, keyframes and Reframe. Keep Flash only when you need legacy fast Ray 2 behavior or old draft economics.

decisionCards.title: Testing edits or crops?
decisionCards.body: Use Flash for older source-video look tests only when the legacy route matters. New edit and crop tests should start on Ray 3.2.

decisionCards.title: Need a fast current draft route?
decisionCards.body: Compare against LTX Fast, Seedance Fast or Veo Fast when speed matters more than preserving a legacy Luma workflow.

referenceWorkflows.title: Text-to-video
referenceWorkflows.body: Keep the draft prompt compact: one subject, one move, one mood and one format.

referenceWorkflows.title: Start image
referenceWorkflows.body: Use a first frame to lock composition while Flash tests motion and pacing.

referenceWorkflows.title: Optional end frame
referenceWorkflows.body: Add an end image only when the active image-to-video route exposes it and the draft needs a specific landing composition.

referenceWorkflows.title: Modify
referenceWorkflows.body: Explore source-video look changes before committing the winning direction to Ray 2.

referenceWorkflows.title: Reframe
referenceWorkflows.body: Validate mobile, square or wide crops while the source clip is still being approved.

demo.title: Demo prompt — Luma Ray 2 Flash
demo.prompt:
5s Luma Ray 2 Flash draft in 16:9, silent output.
A futuristic sports car enters a wet tunnel and reveals its silhouette in a band of light.
Short push-in, low stable camera, fast asphalt reflections, clean contrast.
One motion beat, teaser energy, no readable text, no added logo.
Use Flash to validate direction and timing before a Ray 2 pass.

summary.subject: Sports car in a wet tunnel
summary.action: Enters the light and reveals its silhouette
summary.style: Cinematic draft, fast reflections, clean contrast
summary.output: Silent draft, ready to compare against Ray 2
